Patch Set 2:

From the failed test log:
04:37:00:Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == sanity test 129: test directory size limit ========================== 04:36:59 (1358858219)
04:37:00:Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: dv=$(lctl get_param -n *.lustre-MDT0000.mntdev);
04:37:00:if foo=$(lvdisplay -c $dv 2>/dev/null); then
04:37:00:    echo dm-${foo##*:};
04:37:00:else
04:37:00:    echo $(basename $dv);
04:37:00:fi;
04:37:00:Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: test -e /proc/fs/ldiskfs/dm-0/max_dir_size
04:37:00:Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: echo 4096 >/sys/fs/ldiskfs/dm-0/max_dir_size
04:37:01:LustreError: 15712:0:(osd_handler.c:2320:osd_object_ref_del()) ASSERTION( (oh)->ot_declare_ref_del > 0 ) failed: 
04:37:01:LustreError: 15712:0:(osd_handler.c:2320:osd_object_ref_del()) LBUG
04:37:01:Pid: 15712, comm: mdt00_004

Looks very related to this change.

Patch Set 2:

Well that was not readable.  

04:37:00:Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: echo 4096 >/sys/fs/ldiskfs/dm-0/max_dir_size

04:37:01:LustreError: 15712:0:(osd_handler.c:2320:osd_object_ref_del()) ASSERTION( (oh)->ot_declare_ref_del > 0 ) failed:

Patch Set 2:

Thnx Keith, I can see this ASSERT. Probably this bug does not relate to this patch, and exist before. I think, mdd_create() cleanup have invalid error processing at this part
cleanup:
        if (rc != 0 && created != 0) {
Now I`m try to investigate it.

Patch Set 10: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

I don't understand why these extra patches are included in this patch?

Patch Set 10: Verified

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/de918620-a8c0-11e2-a141-52540035b04c).  Ran 14 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 10:

Andreas that is due to the new option Opt_max_dir_size_kb being added. Since the ext4 options vary greatly across the distros lustre support any changes has a big impact.

Patch Set 10: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

I think this patch is unnecessarily large because of the move of the max_dir_size patch within all of the patch series changing the context of the intervening patches, but that isn't the end of the world I guess.

Another tip for keeping patches smaller is to avoid adding all of the new options at the end of the option list.  This avoids conflict between patches, and also conflict with changes landed in the upstream code.

Patch Set 10:

I have thinking about what you are saying and have come up with a better way to handle this. This patch attempted to cover all distributions with one core patch. As we support more and more distributions this will become more difficult.
What needs to be done is that create a patch just to handle these option list. We still can share a core patch but have a extra patch for each platform. Eventually we can add more to these special options patches but that is more a 2.5 thing.
Patch Set 10: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve
Uploaded patch set 11.

Patch Set 11:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/15442/

Patch Set 11: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/15442/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 11:

Toro has started the test process

Patch Set 11: Fails

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/4153133a-bc3c-11e2-96d6-52540035b04c).  Ran 14 tests.  2 tests failed: conf-sanity, sanity.

Patch Set 11: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

I'm supportive of splitting the patch into a generic and a distro-specific chunk (we previously did this with mballoc patches before they were merged).  However, these patches should be kept together instead of pitting the "ext4-options" patch at the end if the series. That will likely result in the ext4-options patch having to be refreshed for every distro whenever an earlier patch is changed, and will not result in reduced patch maintenance.

I'd rather see  "ext4-max-dir-size" and "ext4-max-dir-size-rhel6.4" and they are kept together in the patch series.  It will also make it much more clear to the next developer that these two patches are related, which is not at all clear in the current version.

If necessary to avoid a lot of other patch updates, the "ext4-max-dir-size" patch could be moved to the end of the series.

Patch Set 11:

I got ahead of myself. I really like to see ext4-option.patch become the patch for all the Opt_XX. This way more patches down the road can be shared since the most common reason for conflicts is the Opt_XX in super.c. I guess we can wait until 2.5 to deal with that issue.
Uploaded patch set 12.

parents.subject: LU-3264 tests: support failover testing with ZFS

commit.subject: LU-2479 ldiskfs: fix max_dir_size checking
commit.message:
LU-2479 ldiskfs: fix max_dir_size checking

This patch fix max_dir_size checking and adds max_dir_size_kb
mount options according to ext4 main line patch
df981d03eeff7971ac7e6ff37000bfa702327ef1. Sanity test 129
changed to check directory size limits.

1) file creation without directory size increase
2) file creation after directory limit
3) file creation with disable limit

Signed-off-by: Alexander Boyko <alexander_boyko@xyratex.com>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Xyratex-bug-id: MRP-697
Change-Id: I3b8502c0b1fa77622a4a0da6ef8d33d4f8194f69
